core_model.structure_summary: A finite system of first-order ordinary differential equations in which each unit's velocity depends only on its gap to the next unit through a nonlinear monotone function, coupled in a nearest-neighbor chain.

variables.meaning: position of each ordered unit along a line, or equivalently the reciprocal gap between consecutive units
fixed spacing scale and the monotone velocity-versus-density response function

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A scalar first-order conservation law in one space dimension with nonlinear flux, obtained as the continuum limit of the nearest-neighbor chain, whose entropy weak solutions develop shocks.

notes: Two equally central objects are linked: the discrete nearest-neighbor ODE chain (taken as core) and the scalar conservation law PDE (taken as its continuum/mean-field skeleton). The paper's thesis is precisely that the former is a numerical scheme converging to the latter, so the discrete-to-continuum relationship is the natural core/skeleton split. A Lagrangian conservation law y_t - V(y)_x = 0 also appears as an intermediate form.
